name: Okinawa
summary: Okinawa is exceptionally safe with extremely low crime rates and excellent public safety. The main risks are natural disasters like typhoons and earthquakes, though the prefecture has strong emergency response systems. Visitors enjoy very high safety levels day and night.
quickVerdict: Okinawa stands out as one of Japan's safest destinations with violent crime being virtually non-existent and petty crime extremely rare. The prefecture maintains excellent safety standards both during the day and at night. The primary concerns for travelers are natural hazards during typhoon season (June to November) and occasional earthquakes, though Japan's world-class emergency response systems provide robust protection. Women feel exceptionally safe traveling alone, and the transport infrastructure is reliable and secure.

neighborhoods.name: Naha Central
neighborhoods.description: The capital's downtown area around Kokusai Street is very safe with excellent police presence and well-lit streets. Heavy tourist activity ensures constant foot traffic and security.

neighborhoods.name: American Village
neighborhoods.description: This entertainment district in Chatan is generally safe but can get crowded during nightlife hours. Minor pickpocketing risks exist in busy areas, but violent crime remains extremely rare.

neighborhoods.name: Northern Okinawa
neighborhoods.description: Rural areas and beaches in the north are exceptionally safe with virtually no crime. Main concerns are natural hazards and limited emergency services access in remote locations.

neighborhoods.name: Military Base Areas
neighborhoods.description: Areas around US military installations are generally safe but historically have higher incident rates. Exercise normal precautions and avoid restricted zones.

neighborhoods.name: Tsuji District
neighborhoods.description: Naha's historic red-light district requires moderate caution during late night hours. While not dangerous, it's advisable to be aware of surroundings after midnight.

neighborhoods.name: Remote Islands
neighborhoods.description: Outer islands like Ishigaki and Miyako are extremely safe with minimal crime. Main concerns are limited medical facilities and weather-related transport disruptions.

scams.name: Restaurant Overcharging
scams.description: Some tourist-area restaurants may add hidden charges or inflate bills, particularly around popular beaches and Kokusai Street.
scams.howToAvoid: Check prices before ordering, ask for itemized receipts, and research restaurant reviews beforehand.

scams.name: Unauthorized Tour Guides
scams.description: Unlicensed individuals may offer expensive tours with poor service or misleading information, especially near major attractions.
scams.howToAvoid: Only book tours through licensed operators, verify credentials, and check online reviews before paying.

scams.name: Currency Exchange Scams
scams.description: Some exchange services may offer poor rates or add hidden fees, reducing your money's value.
scams.howToAvoid: Use bank ATMs or official exchange counters, compare rates, and avoid street money changers.

scams.name: Accommodation Tax Confusion
scams.description: The new 2% accommodation tax may not be clearly explained, leading to unexpected charges at checkout.
scams.howToAvoid: Confirm total costs including taxes when booking, ask hotels to explain all fees upfront.

soloFemale.overview: Okinawa is exceptionally safe for solo female travelers with very low harassment rates and a respectful culture. Women regularly travel alone without incident, and locals are helpful and courteous. Public areas are well-maintained and well-lit, making night travel generally comfortable.
tips: Exercise normal precautions around military base areas where alcohol-related incidents are more common
Use women-only cars on the monorail during rush hours if available
Stay in well-reviewed accommodations and inform someone of your itinerary for remote island visits

nightSafety.overview: Okinawa maintains excellent night safety with well-lit streets, regular police patrols, and low crime rates. Most areas remain safe for walking after dark, though some caution is advised in entertainment districts during peak hours. The prefecture's strong community culture contributes to overall security.
tips: Stick to well-lit main streets and avoid isolated beaches or remote areas after dark
Be aware of your surroundings in entertainment districts like American Village during weekend nights
Keep emergency contacts handy and inform accommodation of late return plans

transport.metro: The Yui Rail monorail is extremely safe, clean, and punctual, connecting Naha Airport to central Naha and Shuri in 40 minutes. Security cameras and regular patrols ensure passenger safety.
transport.rideshare: Rideshare services are limited in Okinawa. Traditional taxi services are very reliable and safe, though more expensive than mainland Japan.
transport.taxis: Taxis are abundant, safe, and professionally operated with standardized rates. Drivers are courteous and vehicles well-maintained. Airport shuttle services are particularly reliable.
transport.tips: Rental cars are the most flexible option for exploring beyond Naha. Public buses are safe but infrequent. Always use licensed taxis with proper identification.

customs: Remove shoes when entering homes, traditional restaurants, and any building with tatami mats
Bow slightly as a greeting and show of respect, though handshakes are acceptable for foreigners
Avoid eating or drinking while walking; find a designated area to stop and consume food or beverages

health.overview: Okinawa has excellent healthcare with modern medical facilities and well-trained staff. Air quality is generally good, and food safety standards are high throughout the prefecture.
health.water: Tap water is completely safe to drink throughout Okinawa and meets strict Japanese safety standards. Bottled water is widely available but unnecessary.
health.vaccinations: No special vaccinations required. Routine vaccinations should be up to date. Consider hepatitis A and Japanese encephalitis for extended rural stays.
health.altitude: Okinawa's subtropical climate features high humidity and strong UV rays. Heat exhaustion is possible in summer months. Typhoon season runs June through November.

emergency.usEmbassy: US Consulate Naha: 098-876-4211. Main embassy in Tokyo: 03-3224-5000. After-hours emergencies: 03-3224-5000

faq.a: Yes, Okinawa is exceptionally safe for tourists with extremely low crime rates and excellent public safety standards. Violent crime against visitors is virtually non-existent, and the prefecture maintains high security levels.

Okinawa is very safe at night with well-lit streets and regular police patrols. Most areas remain comfortable for evening walks, though standard precautions should be taken in entertainment districts.

faq.q: Is Okinawa safe for solo female travelers?
faq.a: Yes, Okinawa is extremely safe for solo female travelers. Women regularly travel alone without incident, harassment rates are very low, and the local culture is respectful and helpful toward visitors.

faq.q: What areas should I avoid in Okinawa?
faq.a: There are no particularly dangerous areas in Okinawa, but exercise moderate caution in the Tsuji district late at night and around military base areas during peak hours. Remote beaches after dark should also be avoided.

faq.q: Is it safe to use public transport in Okinawa?
faq.a: Yes, public transport in Okinawa is very safe and reliable. The monorail system is modern and secure, buses are well-maintained, and taxis are professionally operated with standardized safety measures.
